January 17 | HIGH-CONFIDENCE PICK
NFL (4:30pm EST)

Bills +1.5 (-110) = 2.5U
The Collective Analysis
(Combined reasoning from top cappers)
¢ Recent Road and ATS Performance: Buffalo
enters this game riding strong road form,
winning four straight away games since Week

| 12 and posting a 3-1 record against the spread
in those contests. That stretch includes last
week's 27-24 playoff win at Jacksonville as a
2.5-point underdog, reinforcing that Buffalo

~ consistently stays within tight margins or wins
outright on the road. Getting +1.5 points aligns
directly with a team that has already proven it

Can cover as an underdog in hostile
environments late in the season.

«Defensive Efficiency Edge: Buffalo's passing
defense ranked No. 1 in the NFL, allowing just
2,894 passing yards with opponents
completing only 59.7 percent of attempts,
while giving up 19 passing touchdowns against

' 13 interceptions. That efficiency directly
targets Denver quarterback Bo Nix, who in his
only prior playoff meeting with Buffalo
managed just 144 passing yards despite no
turnovers. Buffalo's ability to limit explosive
passing plays increases the likelihood of alow- _,

margin game where points are ata premium, |*
favoring the Bills with the spread. \
